_comment: cherrypick orchestration config. Copy to config.json and adjust. A module is located by 'repo' (cloned by `cherrypick install` into ~/.cherrypick/modules/<repo-name>) or by an explicit 'path' override; paths are relative to this file's directory. Never hardcode absolute machine paths.
_home_note: Runtime home (this config.json, logs/, state/, dashboard.html) = CHERRYPICK_HOME if set, else the repo root in a source checkout, else ~/.cherrypick for a pip-installed copy. An installed copy thus reads ~/.cherrypick/config.json.
_modules_home_note: Managed module checkouts live under CHERRYPICK_MODULES_HOME, else CHERRYPICK_HOME/modules, else ~/.cherrypick/modules. `install` clones a module only when its checkout is absent and never touches an existing one; `uninstall` leaves checkouts in place.

meic._path_note: Locate the module by EITHER 'repo' (install clones it to the managed modules home) OR 'path' (an in-place dev checkout, absolute or relative to this file — never cloned over). Optional 'ref' picks a branch/tag to clone.
meic._keyring_note: The module's OS-keyring service name, used by `cherrypick account`/`connect` to read/select the module's live-trading account (ACCOUNT_NUMBER) via the shared CredentialStore. Optional 'keyring_legacy_services' lists read-only fallbacks (MEIC keeps 'tastytrade-mcp' for pre-rename creds). Omit to disable account selection for this module.

paper._paper_db_note: Path to the module's paper-trades SQLite DB — MUST point at the file the module actually WRITES (a mismatch silently blinds the orchestrator's report/reconcile/dashboard and the watchdog freshness check). Relative resolves against the module checkout; a leading '~' or env var expands (use e.g. '~/.cherrypick/data/meic/paper_trades.db' when the module keeps its data in the managed home); an absolute path is used as-is.

streamer._stop_argv_note: Optional. Used to stop a STALLED streamer before relaunching it — the daemon is single-instance guarded, so relaunching without stopping is a silent no-op. Defaults to the status command with --stop.
streamer._stale_restart_note: Restart the streamer when it is CONNECTED but has received no market events for this many seconds during RTH. A process check cannot see this: observed 2026-07-20, the streamer reconnected then went silent for 8 minutes while still reporting running=true and its own stale_warning=false (that flag only trips at 600s), so nothing restarted it — MEIC degraded to REST and the flies module refused every iteration on stale quotes. 240s is well inside the 600s self-report and well outside a normal quiet tick. Restarts are skipped while a connection is younger than this, so a just-restarted streamer is not judged before it has resubscribed.

calibration._comment: Optional. `cherrypick calibrate` reads paper P&L per risk profile and advises promotion up this ladder (advisory only; never switches live risk). Omit 'ladder' to get readings without recommendations. 'deliberate_only' rungs are never auto-recommended; 'rule' overrides the default {min_days:14, min_win_rate:0.6, min_sample:20}.

flies._comment: cherrypick-flies: 0DTE net-credit butterflies ('profit forest'), paper only, SPX only. Reads MEIC's stream cache READ-ONLY and runs no streamer of its own, so it REQUIRES the meic module's streamer to be running and subscribed to SPX. ENABLED, but it has still never completed a live session — watch the first one, in particular the quote_stats line in the log (which separates 'the streamer gave us nothing' from 'the strategy found nothing') and the arm_divergence figure (if the arms agree nearly always, the comparison cannot answer anything and max_center_distance_pct is the first thing to revisit).

paper._kind_note: self_healing, NOT cherrypick_scheduled. This was cherrypick_scheduled, which registers one entry task at entry_time and one exit task at exit_time — a shape that fits Earnings (one entry, one exit per day) and is wrong for a legged 0DTE strategy. A single daily entry would open a credit spread at noon and never look at it again, so the completing spread could never be bought and the completion rate — the module's headline measurement — would read 0% every day for reasons that have nothing to do with the market. cherrypick_scheduled also hardcodes the earnings launcher verbs, so this module's own entry_argv would have been ignored entirely.
paper._timing_note: The module registers its own recurring task (every 2 min) and `--once` carries all the session logic: out of RTH it is a clean no-op, after the settle time it settles the day and writes the EOD reports exactly once. One task, so there is no second schedule to drift out of sync with the first.

calibration._comment: The 'ladder' here is not a risk ladder — it is the three experiment arms, and there is nothing to promote between them. Listing them gives per-arm P&L readings without recommendations, which is exactly what this module wants: the comparison is the output.

paper._dolt_databases_note: Databases the runner needs served on dolt_host:dolt_port. doctor verifies each is present (not just that the port answers) when a MySQL client is available, catching a Dolt server rooted at the wrong data dir. Omit to check port reachability only.
paper._dolt_service_note: Optional cherrypick-managed keep-alive. `install` registers a MINUTE task that runs `ensure-dolt` (starts `dolt sql-server` from data_dir only when nothing is on the port); `uninstall` removes it; `status`/`doctor` surface it. data_dir is portable: `~` expands, a relative path resolves against the cherrypick runtime ROOT. Omit this block to manage Dolt yourself.

watchdog._drawdown_note: Optional paper-P&L drift alert (net-of-cost). Add a 'drawdown' block to enable: {"suite_floor": -1000, "module_floors": {"meic": -750, "earnings": -750}, "critical_multiplier": 2}. net<=floor -> WARN; net<=floor*critical_multiplier -> CRITICAL. Paper only; never trades. Omit to disable.

dashboard._comment: Read-only status dashboard: static self-contained HTML regenerated on each watchdog tick and by `cherrypick dashboard`. Surfaces suite health + P&L + logs, plus a System panel (scheduled tasks, installed modules, and a redacted config summary — webhook state only, never URLs). Under --serve it adds a live checks card that polls doctor.run() (the only broker-touching part, kept off the static path). 'output' is relative to this file's directory.

serve._comment: `cherrypick dashboard --serve` runs a localhost live view (same page rebuilt per request) instead of writing the static file. Loopback-only, read-only. Override host/port on the CLI with --host/--port.

sections._comment: Optional live cards for `dashboard --serve`. Each section is subprocessed for a cherrypick.core.viz payload and rendered generically (the orchestrator never imports the module). A new module gets a card by emitting the schema — add it here. Locate the module by 'path' (in-place checkout, relative to this file) or 'repo'. fetch_argv may use {symbol} etc. Deliberately NOT under 'modules' (that registry is for paper pipelines).
sections.title: GEX

sections._comment: cherrypick-flies' payoff curve. The viz `bars` contract (signed values, tone_by_sign, a focus marker) was built for net-GEX-by-strike but maps exactly onto payoff-by-price, so this card draws the 'profit forest' itself — green across the band where the book profits, red outside it, focus at spot — with no flies-specific rendering code here.
sections.title: Flies

embeds._comment: Optional embedded module dashboards for `dashboard --serve` only: each module's OWN full dashboard, shown in an iframe on the orchestrator page. The orchestrator drives the module by config-declared argv (never imports it) and PAPER mode must be forced in that argv. Locate the module by 'path' (relative to this file) or 'repo'. Two kinds: 'server' (module runs its own HTTP dashboard — orchestrator launches it if the port is down, iframe points at {port}) and 'static' (module regenerates a self-contained HTML file — orchestrator runs build_argv, throttled to refresh_seconds, and serves 'output'). Disabled by default.
embeds.title: MEIC

embeds._comment: The gex module's full GEX/IV-Skew/Volume dashboard (read-only off the shared stream cache). Complements the compact live GEX section card above.
embeds.title: GEX Dashboard

embeds._comment: cherrypick-flies' full dashboard: the live payoff curve, open positions with their post-fee floors, the decision journal (why each entry was made or refused), and P&L / win rate / completion rate over timeframes. Read-only over the paper DB, loopback-only, no broker and no network. Paper is the only mode this module has, so there is no --mode flag to force.
embeds.title: Flies

services._comment: Long-running background daemons the orchestrator keeps alive: started at `install`, restarted by the watchdog if down (single-instance guarded), stopped at `uninstall`. Located by 'path' (relative to this file) or 'repo'. status_argv must print {"running": bool}. The gex spot-trail recorder samples every symbol's spot continuously so the GEX dashboard's spot trail has no gaps and builds all session, independent of whether the dashboard is open. Disabled by default.

MEIC's post-cutover sidecar: the REST account poller + the 127.0.0.1:7699 HTTP API ONLY, no DXLink streaming (the standalone `streamer` above is the market-data producer). It serves the shared cache read-only and caches account/positions/market-overview into MEIC's own rest_cache.db, so MEIC's interactive loop keeps its REST/quote fast-path without a second writer on the shared cache. Optional and OFF by default — MEIC works without it (quotes read the cache directly; account calls just go live). Enable it if you run MEIC's live/interactive loop and want the cached fast-path back.

streamer._comment: The suite's standalone market-data producer (packages/streamer) — the single streamer that writes the shared cache ~/.cherrypick/data/marketdata/stream_cache.db, which every module reads. DISABLED until the cutover: today MEIC owns the streamer under modules.meic.streamer, and exactly ONE producer runs at a time. At the flip, set enabled=true here AND modules.meic.streamer.enabled=false (MEIC becomes a sidecar that keeps only its REST poller + 7699 API). Session-gated and silence-restarted by the watchdog, the same contract MEIC's streamer has. Located by 'path' (relative to this file). Modules declare which symbols they need via ~/.cherrypick/state/stream_requests/<module>.json; the streamer streams the union.

trade_notify._comment: Dedicated low-latency task that pushes new paper entries/exits to trade_channels. Runs far more often than the watchdog so fills reach you within ~interval_minutes instead of a full watchdog cycle.

eod_digest._comment: Suite end-of-day digest: writes logs/eod-digest-<day>.md (one session's cross-module paper P&L, net of costs, plus a pointer to each module's own paper-eod file) and pushes a one-line summary through your notify channels. EVENT-DRIVEN (no fixed clock time): the watchdog fires it once EVERY installed module has written its paper-eod-<day>.md, so it can't race a module that settles a few minutes late (the old fixed 16:15 missed flies when it settled at 16:20). 'deadline' (ET, box-local clock) is the backstop - if some module is late or never writes (a flat flies session writes none), the digest fires anyway at the deadline with whatever exists, so it never skips. ON by default; OPT OUT with enabled=false then re-run `install` (removes the trigger's effect by disabling) or `uninstall`. 'task_name' is kept only so install/uninstall can remove a stale fixed-time task from a prior install. Read-only over the paper DBs; fired detached, off the watchdog reliability path.

log_archive._comment: End-of-month log/report rotation (`cherrypick archive`). A monthly scheduled task zips each FINISHED month's dated reports (paper-eod / eod-analysis / eod-digest / live eod) and rotated log backups (*.log.N) into logs/archive/<YYYY-MM>/<scope>.zip - one zip per scope (the suite logs root + each module dir) - then removes the originals once the zip verifies. Idempotent and safe: it never touches the current month or an active .log, and re-runs converge. ON by default and registered by `install`; OPT OUT with enabled=false then re-run `install`/`uninstall`. 'day' is the day of month it fires (1..28), 'at' the box's local clock. Files only; off the watchdog reliability path. Run on demand any time with `cherrypick archive` (add --dry-run to preview, --month YYYY-MM to scope to one month).

eod_insight._comment: AI EOD insight (`cherrypick eod-insight`). When enabled AND Claude Code (`claude`) is on PATH, pipes the day's DETERMINISTIC reports (each module's eod-analysis + paper-eod, and the suite digest) to Claude in headless print mode and writes a synthesized cross-module narrative to logs/eod-insight-<day>.md (linked from the dashboard EOD card). EVENT-DRIVEN: the watchdog launches it DETACHED on the same completion event as the digest (all modules' paper-eod present, or the digest deadline) - no fixed clock time - so the AI call never runs in the watchdog process. Claude runs with NO execution/edit/arbitrary-URL tools (Bash/Edit/Write/WebFetch/Task always disallowed) and never touches the broker or config - the orchestrator writes the file. 'research_events' (default true) additionally grants WebSearch ONLY, so the insight can research upcoming macro events (FOMC/CPI/jobs) and highly anticipated earnings for a forward-looking section (anchored on the suite's deterministic FOMC/expiry/holiday calendar); set false for a purely offline, reports-only synthesis. OFF by default overall (needs Claude + an authenticated session + a paid API call, and research adds web calls + latency); the deterministic eod-analysis remains the source of record and researched notes are best-effort. Off the watchdog reliability path; best-effort. Turn on with enabled=true then re-run `install`. 'model' null uses Claude's configured default. Paper/educational only, not financial advice.

reconcile._comment: `cherrypick reconcile` (and the serve-only /api/reconcile dashboard card): a paper<->live isolation guard. It enumerates EVERY account on the login (list_accounts - tastytrade returns multiple per user) and checks each (read-only get_positions/get_account_info), flagging any open positions/BP a paper-only suite shouldn't have. On-demand and off the watchdog reliability path; never trades; account numbers masked. Optional 'broker_module' forces which module's tt.py to query; omit to auto-discover the first positions-capable one.

notify._channels_note: push channels: 'log' (always on, the floor), 'desktop', 'slack', 'discord'. Slack/Discord webhook URLs live in the OS keyring (cherrypick secrets-set --channel slack|discord), never in this file or env vars.
